record.field: LLDAP is a lightweight LDAP server with a web UI to centralize users and groups for self-hosted apps — a simple alternative to OpenLDAP and Active Directory.

LLDAP is a lightweight LDAP server with a web UI to centralize users and groups for self-hosted apps — a simple alternative to OpenLDAP and Active Directory.

It gives you a single directory of users and groups that your internal applications can authenticate against over LDAP, without the complexity of running a full OpenLDAP or Active Directory deployment. Written in Rust, it ships as one small binary with an opinionated, simplified schema and a clean web interface for managing accounts and memberships. It targets home labs, small teams, and self-hosters who want centralized identity for tools like Authelia, Nextcloud, Grafana, or Gitea while keeping the moving parts to a minimum.

Key features
  • Simple, opinionated LDAP schema focused on users, groups, and memberships
  • Clean web interface to create and manage users, groups, and attributes
  • LDAP bind for application authentication, plus LDAPS when certificates are configured
  • Built-in GraphQL API for automation and scripting
  • Documented integrations with Authelia, Authentik, Nextcloud, Grafana, Gitea, and many more
  • Lightweight single Rust binary with a tiny memory and CPU footprint
Best for

Home labs, small teams, and self-hosters who need one central directory for their internal apps without operating a full OpenLDAP or Active Directory. Keep the LDAP port on trusted networks, since plain LDAP is unencrypted unless you enable LDAPS.

services: lldap: image: lldap/lldap:stable restart: unless-stopped environment: LLDAP_JWT_SECRET: ${LLDAP_JWT_SECRET} LLDAP_KEY_SEED: ${LLDAP_KEY_SEED} LLDAP_LDAP_BASE_DN: ${LLDAP_LDAP_BASE_DN} LLDAP_LDAP_USER_PASS: ${LLDAP_LDAP_USER_PASS} volumes: - ./lldap_data:/data ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:17170' - '{{ PORT_LDAP }}:3890' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
Secret used to sign session JWT tokens.
Seed used to derive the server encryption key; keep it stable across restarts.
Password for the built-in LDAP administrator account.
Base distinguished name (DN) for the LDAP directory tree.
